steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Hew out is used figuratively to describe attempts by individuals at a gathering to express their ideas about art.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The figurative phrase "hew out" (to chop or carve out with effort) is used for trying to articulate a personal conception of art. The word needs to mean express or clarify. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Visualize: to form a mental image — suggests picturing art rather than putting it into words for others; the speakers are trying to communicate, not just imagine. ✗
  • Choice B — Define: to state the meaning of clearly — fits the metaphor of carving out a clear articulation of one's conception of art. ✓
  • Choice C — Contract: to make smaller or enter an agreement — neither meaning fits an attempt to express an artistic idea. ✗
  • Choice D — Emulate: to imitate or try to match — implies copying someone else's conception of art, not expressing one's own. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
